steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two independent clauses: one about travelers flying out of urban airports with increasing frequency, and one giving San Diego as a specific example. The blank must punctuate the end of the first clause and introduce the example correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'frequency, for example.': A comma cannot join two independent clauses, and ending with a period leaves the second sentence without a subject opener. ✗
  • Choice B — 'frequency, for example;': A semicolon after 'for example' would separate the transitional phrase from the clause it introduces, creating a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — 'frequency. For example,': A period ends the first independent clause cleanly. 'For example,' then opens the second sentence as a transitional phrase followed by a comma — the standard punctuation pattern. ✓
  • Choice D — 'frequency, for example,': Two commas around 'for example' while joining two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
